item.title: 【Mastering the AWS Well-Architected AI Stack: A Deep Dive into ML, GenAI, and Sustainability Lenses】掌握 AWS 架构完善的 AI 堆栈：深入探讨 ML、GenAI 和可持续发展镜头
item.description:
【

As Artificial Intelligence (AI) shifts from experimental prototypes to mission-critical production systems, the complexity of managing these workloads has grown exponentially. Organizations no longer just need models that work; they need systems that are secure, cost-effective, reliable, and sustainable.

To address this, AWS has expanded its Well-Architected Framework with specialized "Lenses." For technical architects and lead engineers, three lenses are now critical: the Machine Learning (ML) Lens, the Generative AI Lens, and the Sustainability Lens.

】

随着人工智能 (AI) 从实验原型转变，管理这些工作负载的复杂性呈指数级增长。组织不再仅仅需要有效的模型；还需要有效的模型。他们需要安全、经济高效、可靠且可持续的系统。

为了解决这个问题，AWS 通过专门的“镜头”扩展了其架构完善的框架。对于技术架构师和首席工程师来说，现在三个镜头至关重要：机器学习 (ML) 镜头、生成式 AI 镜头和可持续发展镜头。

item.title: 【Hot Data: Where Real-Time Insight Begins】热门数据：实时洞察的起点
item.description:
【

Hot data means the data currently being created, accessed, and queried in real-time or near real-time. The latest and most time-critical data, such as live events, user interactions, sensor measurements, or transaction streams, often require the processing to be right away and latency to be low. 

Hot (or warm for Gradient Data) has the greatest short-term value, so it is often kept in fast or streaming systems that are designed to process and return data very rapidly to provide instant insights and make lightning decisions.

】

热数据是指当前正在实时或近实时创建、访问和查询的数据。最新且最关键的数据（例如实时事件、用户交互、传感器测量或事务流）通常要求立即处理且延迟较低。 

热（或梯度数据的暖）具有最大的短期价值，因此它通常保存在快速或流式系统中，这些系统旨在非常快速地处理和返回数据，以提供即时见解并做出闪电决策。

item.title: 【Rethinking Java Web UIs With Jakarta Faces and Quarkus】使用 Jakarta Faces 和 Quarkus 重新思考 Java Web UI
item.description:
【

Nowadays, Java enterprise applications often default to Angular, React, or Vue for the frontend. But for this kind of application, the most natural UI framework already exists in the Java ecosystem: Jakarta Faces.

Modern Java enterprise applications tend to follow a familiar pattern: a Java backend exposing REST APIs and a JavaScript/TypeScript frontend built with some library like Angular, React, or Vue. This architecture has become so standard that we rarely question it.

】

如今，Java 企业应用程序通常默认使用 Angular、React 或 Vue 作为前端。但对于此类应用，Java 生态系统中已经存在最自然的 UI 框架：Jakarta Faces。

现代 Java 企业应用程序往往遵循一种熟悉的模式：公开 REST API 的 Java 后端和使用 Angular、React 或 Vue 等库构建的 JavaScript/TypeScript 前端。这种架构已经变得如此标准，以至于我们很少质疑它。

item.title: 【End-to-End Automation Using Microsoft Playwright CLI】使用 Microsoft Playwright CLI 的端到端自动化
item.description:
【

With the rapid adoption of AI coding agents such as Claude Code and GitHub Copilot, browser automation tools must prioritize efficiency and scalability. Traditional protocols like MCP (Model Context Protocol) often flood the model’s context window with verbose data, such as full accessibility trees and page structure metadata. This leads to degraded performance, increased costs, and lost reasoning context.

What's Covered in This Blog

  • The article provides a comprehensive and formal installation guide.
  • Complete the setup process in a clear, step-by-step manner.
  • Execution workflow with detailed instructions.
  • Fully implemented end-to-end practical demonstration.
  • Demonstration is performed using the site's online store
  • A detailed walkthrough VIDEO is attached at the end of the article for additional reference and clarity.

Why Separate Playwright CLI?

Traditional AI-driven browser automation often relies on MCP (Model Context Protocol). While MCP provides rich browser introspection, it introduces a critical limitation: the server controls what enters the model’s context.

】

随着 Claude Code 和 GitHub Copilot 等 AI 编码代理的快速采用，浏览器自动化工具必须优先考虑效率和可扩展性。 MCP（模型上下文协议）等传统协议通常会用详细数据填充模型的上下文窗口，例如完整的可访问性树和页面结构元数据。这会导致性能下降、成本增加以及推理上下文丢失。

本博客涵盖的内容

  • 本文提供了全面且正式的安装指南。
  • 以清晰、分步的方式完成设置过程。
  • 包含详细说明的执行工作流程。
  • 全面实施端到端实际演示。
  • 使用网站的在线商店进行演示
  • 文章末尾附有详细的演练视频，以供额外参考和清晰说明。

为什么要单独使用 Playwright CLI？

传统的人工智能驱动的浏览器自动化通常依赖于MCP（模型上下文协议）。虽然 MCP 提供了丰富的浏览器自省功能，但它引入了一个关键限制：服务器控制进入模型上下文的内容。

item.title: 【Why Retries Are More Dangerous Than Failures】为什么重试比失败更危险
item.description:
【

The instinct is hardwired into every engineer who's shipped production code: if the call fails, try again. It feels responsible — a small buffer against network chaos and flaky backends. But that instinct, unchecked, is how you turn a recoverable hiccup into a four-hour outage that gets the CTO on Slack asking what the hell happened.

I've been in the war room when it happens. A service stumbles — maybe a deployment didn't fully bake, maybe the database hit some lock contention — and suddenly every client in the datacenter decides now is the time to demonstrate grit. What was a localized wobble becomes a stampede. The service that was successfully handling 80% of requests gets buried under 300% of normal traffic, nearly all of it retries. Recovery becomes impossible. The system just thrashes, burning CPU to accomplish nothing.

】

每个交付生产代码的工程师都有一种本能：如果调用失败，请重试。它给人一种负责任的感觉——一个针对网络混乱和不稳定后端的小缓冲区。但这种本能，如果不加控制，就会把一个可恢复的小问题变成四个小时的停机，让 Slack 上的 CTO 询问到底发生了什么。

事情发生时我就在作战室。服务出现问题——也许部署没有完全成熟，也许数据库遇到了一些锁争用——突然之间，数据中心的每个客户端都决定现在是展示毅力的时候了。局部晃动变成了踩踏。成功处理 80% 请求的服务被 300% 的正常流量淹没，几乎所有请求都会重试。恢复变得不可能。系统只是不停地颠簸，烧毁CPU，却一事无成。

item.title: 【Backlog Black Hole: Engineering a Semantic Triage Engine at Scale】积压黑洞：大规模设计语义分类引擎
item.description:
【

Our bug tracker manages more than 150 million issues. It’s growing at 20% compounding annually. Roughly 25% of issues are duplicates. That is approximately 35 million issues and growing. Due to the large amount of duplicates in the system, it takes an enormous amount of time to go over them. This results in huge productivity loss. Last quarter, this led to hundreds of duplicate issues being triaged separately, even when they shared the same root cause. Engineers spent days re-investigating problems that had already been diagnosed elsewhere. Keyword search helps, but most of the time, it lacks in surfacing issues that are not an exact match, but are semantically similar.

As ticket volume increased, manual triage became an absolute mess and cumbersome. Incoming issues were categorized independently by different teams, with no reliable mechanism to detect semantic overlap. We observed multiple reports describing the same failure using different surface language, such as transport-layer timeouts versus UI authentication hangs, which were treated as unrelated by the keyword index. Because ownership was assigned per ticket rather than per underlying defect, these reports diverged into separate investigation paths. The result was duplicated debugging effort and delayed resolution, even when the root cause was already understood elsewhere in the system.

】

我们的错误跟踪器管理超过 1.5 亿个问题。它每年以 20% 的复合速度增长。大约 25% 的问题是重复的。大约有 3500 万个问题，并且还在不断增加。由于系统中存在大量重复项，因此需要花费大量时间来检查它们。这导致生产力的巨大损失。上个季度，这导致数百个重复问题被单独分类，即使它们具有相同的根本原因。工程师们花了几天时间重新调查其他地方已经诊断出的问题。关键字搜索有帮助，但大多数时候，它无法显示不完全匹配但语义相似的问题。

随着票务量的增加，手动分类变得绝对混乱和麻烦。传入的问题由不同团队独立分类，没有可靠的机制来检测语义重叠。我们观察到多个使用不同表面语言描述相同故障的报告，例如传输层超时与 UI 身份验证挂起，这些报告被关键字索引视为不相关。由于所有权是按票证分配的，而不是按潜在缺陷分配的，因此这些报告分为不同的调查路径。结果是重复的调试工作和延迟的解决，即使根本原因已经在系统的其他地方被理解。

item.title: 【Big Cloud Still Runs Most Containers on VMs; What Does that Mean for the Rest of Us?】大云仍然在虚拟机上运行大多数容器；这对我们其他人意味着什么？
item.description:
【

If bare metal provides the best raw performance, why do hyperscalers still insist on running their own infrastructure on virtual machines? The answer reveals what the companies running the world’s most complex infrastructure really think about cloud architecture.

Research by the analyst firm ReveCom shows how the major cloud providers overwhelmingly deploy their containerized workloads on virtual machines rather than on bare metal servers. In addition to relying primarily on VMs to support their in-house operations (with the exception of Google), they also rely on VMs instead of bare metal to support the services they offer customers. Those findings are based on reviews of documentation and interviews with engineers and executives at AWS, Google, Microsoft, and DigitalOcean.

】

如果裸机提供了最佳的原始性能，为什么超大规模企业仍然坚持在虚拟机上运行自己的基础设施？答案揭示了运行世界上最复杂基础设施的公司对云架构的真正想法。

分析公司 ReveCom 的研究表明，主要云提供商如何绝大多数将其容器化工作负载部署在虚拟机上，而不是裸机服务器上。除了主要依靠虚拟机来支持其内部运营（Google 除外）之外，他们还依靠虚拟机而不是裸机来支持他们为客户提供的服务。这些调查结果基于对文档的审查以及对 AWS、Google、Microsoft 和 DigitalOcean 的工程师和高管的采访。

item.title: 【Unified Intelligence: Mastering the Azure Databricks and Azure Machine Learning Integration】统一智能：掌握 Azure Databricks 和 Azure 机器学习集成
item.description:
【

In the modern enterprise, the divide between data engineering and data science is often a primary bottleneck for innovation. Data engineers live in the world of distributed clusters, Spark, and ETL pipelines, while data scientists thrive in experimental environments, model tracking, and hyperparameter tuning.

Azure provides two powerhouse platforms to address these needs: Azure Databricks and Azure Machine Learning (Azure ML). While they share some overlapping features, their true potential is unlocked when integrated into a single, cohesive ecosystem. This article provides a deep-dive into why and how you should combine these technologies to build a production-grade Big Data ML pipeline.

】

在现代企业中，数据工程和数据科学之间的鸿沟往往是创新的主要瓶颈。数据工程师生活在分布式集群、Spark 和 ETL 管道的世界中，而数据科学家则在实验环境、模型跟踪和超参数调整中茁壮成长。

Azure 提供了两个强大的平台来满足这些需求：Azure Databricks 和 Azure 机器学习 (Azure ML)。虽然它们具有一些重叠的功能，但当集成到一个单一的、有凝聚力的生态系统中时，它们的真正潜力就会被释放出来。本文深入探讨了为什么以及如何结合这些技术来构建生产级大型数据机器学习管道。

item.title: 【Similarity Search on Tabular Data With Natural Language Fields】自然语言字段表格数据的相似性搜索
item.description:
【

With the introduction of the vector data type and the algorithms available in Oracle Machine Learning (OML) starting with Oracle Database 23ai [2], it is now possible to vectorize records — e.g., via PCA — to support both clustering and similarity search. However, these algorithms do not natively handle fields that contain natural language effectively. This limitation is common in real-world scenarios such as CRM systems, where free-text operator notes or customer feedback coexist with structured attributes like customer profiles and product details.

In this article, we present a technique that seamlessly combines numerical, categorical, and natural language fields into a single, unified vector representation of the entire record. The objective is to improve similarity search and clustering accuracy by preserving both the numerical structure of the data and the semantic meaning of its textual content — without relying on rigid, static WHERE filters that can unnecessarily restrict the results returned.

】

从 Oracle Database 23ai 开始引入 Oracle 机器学习 (OML) 中可用的矢量数据类型和算法 [2]，现在可以对记录进行矢量化（例如通过 PCA）以支持聚类和相似性搜索。然而，这些算法本身并不能有效地处理包含自然语言的字段。这种限制在 CRM 系统等现实场景中很常见，其中自由文本操作员注释或客户反馈与客户资料和产品详细信息等结构化属性共存。

在本文中，我们提出了一种技术，可以将数字、分类和自然语言字段无缝组合成整个记录的单一、统一的向量表示。目标是通过保留数据的数字结构及其文本内容的语义来提高相似性搜索和聚类的准确性，而不依赖于严格的、静态的 WHERE 过滤器，因为这些过滤器会不必要地限制返回的结果。

item.title: 【AWS Bedrock vs. SageMaker: Choosing the Right GenAI Stack in 2026】AWS Bedrock 与 SageMaker：2026 年选择正确的 GenAI 堆栈
item.description:
【

By 2026, the landscape of Generative AI has shifted from simple prompt engineering to complex agentic workflows, autonomous RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipelines, and highly specialized small language models (SLMs). For architects and developers building on Amazon Web Services (AWS), the central question remains: Should you use the managed simplicity of Amazon Bedrock or the granular control of Amazon SageMaker?

This article provides a deep-dive technical comparison of these two powerhouses, helping you navigate the trade-offs in performance, cost, and operational overhead.

】

到 2026 年，生成式 AI 的格局已经从简单的提示工程转变为复杂的代理工作流程、自主 RAG（检索增强生成）管道和高度专业化的小语言模型 (SLM)。对于在 Amazon Web Services (AWS) 上构建的架构师和开发人员来说，核心问题仍然是：您应该使用 Amazon Bedrock 的托管简单性还是 Amazon SageMaker 的精细控制？

本文对这两个强大的技术进行了深入的技术比较，帮助您在性能、成本和运营开销之间进行权衡。
